name: Brevin Jordan
headline: Buried Houston tight end returns from injury as deep flier
body:
The flashes are real but the sample is tiny. Across 2023's 10 games Jordan turned 21 targets into 17 catches for 219 yards and 2 scores, posting a sterling 76.2% success rate, 0.833 EPA per play, and 1.45 yards per route run on a 31.6% snap share. Those efficiency marks tease upside, yet his 2024 was a footnote: two games, three targets, seven yards, a 47.5% snap rate that never translated into volume. The production has never matched the athletic profile.

Now 25, Jordan missed all of 2025 with a torn patellar tendon and signed a one-year extension in December. He is being eased back into practice, but with no established ADP and roughly 0% roster rates, he sits at the bottom of dynasty boards. Treat him as a watch-list dart throw who needs an injury ahead of him to matter.
